kgs.license: Public Domain (FDIC open data, BankFind Suite)

schema_adjacency.notes: PASS. Closest training KG is cms-readmissions at 0.05 Jaccard (1 type overlap = State, 0 rel overlap). Wave-5 concern about cfpb-bank-accounts adjacency is DISPROVEN: the cfpb-bank-accounts pair shows 1 type overlap (State), 0 rel overlap, Jaccard 0.0323 — well below the 0.30 gate and far below the hard rule (>=3 types, >=2 rels). Root cause of the false alarm: cfpb-bank-accounts is actually a ConsumerComplaint-centric schema (keyed on ConsumerComplaint with company/issue/product/subproduct rels), NOT a Bank/Branch/Location structural schema. The wave-5 fear was triggered by the KG name, not by the underlying shape.

kgs.ingest_notes: Ingested via scripts/ingest_multitable.py with spec at eval_holdout_v2/multitable_specs/fdic-call-reports.json. Source JSON payloads staged under benchmarks/datasets/holdout_v2_raw/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/ (institutions.json 4298 records, locations.json 78341 records, financials_2024q2.json 4609 records). JSON-to-CSV preprocessor at scripts/fdic_json_to_csv.py. Per-entity row counts: Bank=4298, CallReport=4609, FinancialMetric=87450 (19 metrics per call report; COST_OF_FUNDS OMITTED because FDIC BankFind does not publish a precomputed cost-of-funds field and the interest-bearing-liability denominator is not cleanly resolvable from the /financials payload), State=58, BankClass=6. Branch entity (78341 rows) was DROPPED from the wave-6 ingest and the raw Branch.csv is staged on disk for future re-ingest, both to preempt the Bank->Branch->Location->State adjacency shape that the wave-5 plan flagged (now proven unnecessary by the adjacency gate running against cfpb-bank-accounts) and to halve the triple volume so the ingest fits inside the shared /update rate budget while 5 concurrent holdout-v2 ingests ran in parallel. Triple ingest runs in background via scripts/ingest_multitable.py at batch-size 2000; bulk POSTing was heavily throttled by rate-limit contention with the 4 other parallel agents (effective ~6 POST/min per agent against the 30/min /update budget) and may continue landing triples after this commit. The adjacency gate was unblocked by pre-seeding 13 minimal triples (5 entity-type anchors + 8 relationship exemplars) via /tmp/fdic_bootstrap.py using the deterministic URI scheme from ingest_multitable.py so the gate could run without waiting for the full bulk POST to complete. // Re-check 2026-04-14: graph was already fully loaded at 1,368,743 triples (target 1,369,741) — the wave-6 overnight ingest actually completed successfully despite the reported connection wedge. Drop/re-ingest skipped as unnecessary. Gold regenerated from scratch on the full data: went from 8 questions (T1=4,T2=2,T3=2,T4=0) to 10 questions (T1=4,T2=0,T3=3,T4=3). T2 bucket shortfall (0 of 4 after 20 attempts, all classifier-mismatched to T3/T4) — FDIC metric shape pushes simple counts into the analytical tier. Old gold backed up to /tmp/fdic_gold_old.json.
bankfind_field_substitutions: RBC1RWAJ -> IDT1RWAJR (Tier 1 Risk-Based Capital Ratio; schema-override field missing from live payload)
RBCT1CER -> IDT1CER (CET1 Ratio; schema-override explicitly names IDT1CER as the duplicate alias)
ELNATRR -> ELNATRY (Net Charge-Offs to Avg Loans; annualized variant, schema-override field missing from live payload)
LNLSNETR -> computed(LNLSNET/ASSET*100) (Loans to Assets; schema-override field missing from live payload)

kgs.license: Public Domain (USPTO PatentsView, US federal agency)

schema_adjacency.notes: PASS. Pristine adjacency: max Jaccard 0.0 across all 36 v9 training KGs, zero entity-type overlap, zero relationship overlap. PatentsView's Patent / Inventor / Assignee / CPCClass / Citation / Location shape has no analog in the training set (clinical trials, CMS, CFPB, census, IMDB, etc.). The 'closest' KG listed is arbitrary since every training KG ties at 0.0 — cfpb-bank-accounts happens to come first in the training iteration order.

kgs.ingest_notes: PatentsView 2020-Q1 slice, deterministic seed=42 reservoir sample of 5,000 patents from the 93,569 patents with patent_date between 2020-01-01 and 2020-03-31. Frozen patent ID list at eval_holdout_v2/multitable_specs/patentsview.patent_ids_2020q1_sample.txt. Ingested via scripts/ingest_multitable.py with spec at eval_holdout_v2/multitable_specs/patentsview.json. CSVs staged at /tmp/patentsview_q1_2020/ (Patent=5000, Inventor=14582, Assignee=4890, CPCClass=25447, CPCEdge=44520, Citation=117660, Location=4155). 1,824,841 triples generated; 1,804,938 landed (small delta from empty-attribute trimming). Schema-size floor PASS (6 types, 8 relationships, 256,737 total instances). Citation graph asymmetry: has_outgoing_citation and cites emit 98,644 rather than 117,660 because ~19k edges in the slice are backward citations where the source Patent is outside the 5k sample; Citation entity and citation_targets_patent still emit all 117,660 so the citation edge set is fully preserved. Recursive Patent->Patent cites edge is the distinguishing structural feature not present in any training KG. Gold generation routed through Cerebras qwen-3-235b-a22b-instruct-2507 via a local shim (scripts/q2forge_v2_cerebras.py wrapper, not committed) because the shared OpenRouter and Anthropic billing accounts were both exhausted at gold-gen time; T4 recursive-cites questions were the only tier qwen failed to reliably produce (0/3), all other tiers passed.

kgs.license: CourtListener / Free Law Project — Public Domain (US federal court records; data is US government work). See https://www.courtlistener.com/help/api/rest/

schema_adjacency.notes: Zero overlap with any training KG. Federal court dockets are a novel legal domain in holdout v2.

kgs.ingest_notes: Source is CourtListener REST v4 (Free Law Project / RECAP Archive), not PACER direct. CourtListener is the free, open mirror of PACER — dockets listed here were uploaded to RECAP by practitioners or scraped from court sites. Fetch used per-court parallel pagination (scripts/fetch_courtlistener_dockets.py) because the global date-filtered listing was prohibitively slow on the CL cursor path. Dockets come from 101 active federal district/appellate/specialty courts. Party/attorney data is gated behind a paid CL tier and is NOT included in this KG; ontology covers Docket, Court, Judge, CaseType, NatureOfSuit, Year, DocketSource.

kgs.notes: FY2022 NIH RePORTER REST API fetch (5500 projects), non-clinical filter drops projects whose FullStudySection.name matches /\bclinical\b/i (213 dropped, 5287 kept). Entity types: Project (anchor), Investigator, Institution, StudySection, Agency (NIH IC), State. Six types exceeds the task brief's >=5 requirement. Schema-size floor reports floor_pass=False because system floor requires >=8 relationships; the brief explicitly requires >=5 entity types which is met. Five rels is a deliberate near-miss accepted by the caller per ingest_multitable.py §4 (floor is advisory, not hard-fail).
